description:
Advances: mutagen.cli

Deliverable:
- Wrap the existing `Application.ensure_all_started(:mutagen_ex)` call at `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:236` in a private helper that recognises the archive-context failure tuple `{:error, {:mutagen_ex, {_, ~c"mutagen_ex.app"}}}` (and the `:non_existing` variant) and self-heals before raising.
- Self-heal sequence:
  1. `Mix.Local.append_archives/0` then retry `Application.ensure_all_started(:mutagen_ex)`.
  2. If still erroring, glob `Path.join(Mix.path_for(:archives), "*")`, call `Mix.Local.archive_ebin/1` on each candidate, and `:code.add_pathz/1` the first ebin that contains `mutagen_ex.app`. Retry once more.
  3. If all three attempts fail, route to the structured-error path below.
- Structured-error path (replaces the raw `MatchError` crash): construct a `%MutagenEx.JsonReporter.Report{}` via `base_report(nil)`, populate `details: %{message: "..."}` with archive-install troubleshooting copy, invoke `MutagenEx.JsonReporter.emit_error(report, :runtime_load_failed)`, write to stdout via `MutagenEx.Pipeline.DefaultIo.emit(iodata, 2, %Config{json_path: nil})`, and halt with exit code 2. No new IO surface invented.
- Detection-predicate discipline: gate the repair branch ONLY on the failure-tuple shape returned by `Application.ensure_all_started/1`. Do NOT probe env vars, `Mix.Project.config/0`, or `:code.lib_dir/1` to "guess" archive context. The first attempt's success is the only signal that an existing-and-working adoption path is in use.
- Implementer choice point (document in a code comment): primary repair = `Mix.Local.append_archives/0` (intent-matching, documented in Mix); defensive fallback = the `archive_ebin/1` scan. Both APIs are public in Mix 1.19.5 / OTP 28. Either is sufficient alone if the other regresses across Mix versions; ship both.
- Unit-test seam: either `@doc false def __ensure_runtime__` or a process-dictionary failure override keyed `:mutagen_ensure_runtime_force_failure` (mirroring the existing `mutagen_json_path_project_root` override at line 507). Implementer picks based on what reads cleanest.

Spec touches:
- `.spec/specs/cli.spec.md` — adds `mutagen.cli.r16`, scenarios `s14c` + `s14d`, verification `v11` (already authored by /distill on this branch — implementer flips `v11.execute: false` to `true` once S2's test file lands).

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs --only archive_context` covering two named cases:
  1. `"ensure_runtime/0 succeeds when :mutagen_ex is already loaded (dep-adoption shape)"` — baseline; never enters repair branch.
  2. `"ensure_runtime/0 routes to structured-error JSON on unrecoverable load failure"` — uses the force-failure seam; asserts `aborted: true`, `abort_reason: "runtime_load_failed"`, exit code 2.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: scenarios `mutagen.cli.s14c` (archive context success after self-heal) and `mutagen.cli.s14d` (unrecoverable failure routes to structured JSON, no MatchError). The integration test that exercises s14c end-to-end is owned by S2 (think-pvr.2); S1's unit tests cover the structured-error path of s14d and the no-op-on-dep-path discipline.
- Mechanical: `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean; `mix smoke` green.
- Spec validation: `mix spec.validate` green.

Out of Scope (files):
- `test/integration/archive_install_test.exs` (owned by S2)
- `lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex` (OTP app spec is correct — the bug is purely `:code` path visibility)
- `README.md`, `CHANGELOG.md` (owned by S3)
- Any other `lib/mutagen_ex/*.ex` modules (the repair lives in the Mix task and helpers it calls)
- `mix.exs` (S2 owns the one-line aliases edit)

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- `lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex`
- `test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs`
- `.spec/specs/cli.spec.md` (already partially authored by /distill — implementer may flip v11.execute from `false` to `true` if S2 ships in the same PR; otherwise leave for S2 to flip)

Out of Scope (intent):
- No new third-party deps. The repair uses `Mix.Local.append_archives/0`, `Mix.path_for(:archives)`, `Mix.Local.archive_ebin/1`, and `:code.add_pathz/1` — all stdlib / Mix-internal surface.
- No env-var or config flag gates the fix. Self-heal triggers on the failure tuple; user does nothing.
- No edits to `test/integration/downstream_adoption_test.exs` — its behaviour is the byte-for-byte parallel reference and must remain unchanged.
- Do NOT change the public surface of `Mix.Tasks.Mutagen.run/1` or `run/2`. The repair lives behind `ensure_runtime/0`.
- Do NOT introduce a new abort_reason atom beyond `:runtime_load_failed` (and ensure the existing reason-naming convention in JsonReporter is followed).

Merge gate:
- [ ] `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean
- [ ] Unit tests in `test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs` pass
- [ ] `mix spec.validate` green
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Verification stubs flipped: none

Summary: Added a hidden ensure-runtime seam plus archive-context repair behind the existing run/1 preamble. The first successful Application.ensure_all_started/1 result bypasses repair, archive-context failure tuples trigger append_archives plus targeted ebin fallback, and unrecoverable failures now emit runtime_load_failed JSON through the existing reporter/io path instead of raising MatchError.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: ba464d9f-4457-42a5-ab7c-da056f0ecad6
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex--git/ba464d9f-4457-42a5-ab7c-da056f0ecad6.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 2 (lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex; test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 1 (v11 stays execute:false per ticket — S2 owns archive_install_test.exs)
- Verification command run: `mix test test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs --only archive_context` => 2 tests, 0 failures
- Requirements coverage (must requirements advanced by this stage):
  - mutagen.cli.r16 (no-op-on-dep-adoption-path discipline): covered by test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs:79 ("succeeds when :mutagen_ex is already loaded"). Would fail if ensure_runtime/0 entered the repair branch on the first `{:ok, _}` (would `refute_received {:io, _, _, _}` to fire) or if `:ok` were not returned.
  - mutagen.cli.r16 (structured-error path, no MatchError): covered by test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs:91 ("routes to structured-error JSON on unrecoverable load failure"). Would fail if a MatchError raised (test would crash), if `abort_reason` != "runtime_load_failed", if exit code != 2, if Config.json_path were not cleared to nil, or if `details.message` lacked the `mix archive.uninstall mutagen_ex` troubleshooting copy. Failure-tuple shape used in the test (`{:enoent, ~c"mutagen_ex.app"}`) genuinely exercises the archive_context_failure?/1 predicate, Mix.Local.append_archives/0, and add_first_mutagen_archive_ebin/0 branches.
  - mutagen.cli.r16 (success-after-self-heal, s14c end-to-end): NOT COVERED at S1 — but explicitly out of scope per ticket; S2's integration test (test/integration/archive_install_test.exs) owns that path. v11.execute remains `false`, correctly deferred.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. No new third-party deps (mix.exs untouched). No env/config flag gates the fix; repair triggers solely on failure-tuple shape. test/integration/downstream_adoption_test.exs untouched. Public surface of run/1 and run/2 unchanged. Only one new abort_reason atom (`:runtime_load_failed`) introduced.
- Project verification: `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` clean; `make smoke` => 640 tests, 0 failures (18 excluded).
- Source-claim trace: spec r16 names `Mix.Local.append_archives/0`, `Mix.path_for(:archives)`, `Mix.Local.archive_ebin/1`, `:code.add_pathz/1` — all four appear at mutagen.ex:269/314/318/322 in the exact call sequence the spec describes, and ensure_runtime/1 actually invokes maybe_repair_archive_context → add_first_mutagen_archive_ebin so the call chain is live (not dead code).
- Audit-expanded scope: none.

Notes:
- Advisory: `lib/mutagen_ex/json_reporter.ex` is out-of-scope for this stage, but the `@type abort_reason ::` enum there does not list `:runtime_load_failed`. Runtime behaviour is correct (emit_error/2 only guards `is_atom/1`), but a future Dialyzer pass on JsonReporter would flag the gap. Treating as advisory because the ticket explicitly forbids editing `lib/mutagen_ex/*.ex` modules and the runtime contract holds.
- `mix spec.validate` is not a defined Mix task in this repo (no spec-led-development tooling installed). Ticket lists it as a merge-gate item but it cannot run here; smoke + archive_context tests + warnings-as-errors compile substitute.
- Implementer chose BOTH seams (`__ensure_runtime__/1` and `:mutagen_ensure_runtime_force_failure` process-dict override). Ticket says "implementer picks based on what reads cleanest"; using both is explicitly allowed and the test seam-injection pattern reads cleanly.

Verdict: PASS

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: f6817170-50ba-42d7-a1a7-b823900b34ba
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex--git/f6817170-50ba-42d7-a1a7-b823900b34ba.jsonl

Reasoning: Re-counted diff scope from base 38e970b: 2 files (lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex, test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs), both in Allowed globs, none Out-of-Scope — verifier's mechanical counts hold. Re-ran `mix test test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs --only archive_context` (2/2 pass), `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` (clean), and `make smoke` (640/0) — all confirm. For r16's structured-error contract (s14d), the failure test at test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs:91 seeds the override with [failure, failure, failure] which forces all three attempts to fail and genuinely exercises archive_context_failure?/1, Mix.Local.append_archives/0, and add_first_mutagen_archive_ebin/0 in sequence; it would fail if a MatchError raised, if abort_reason != "runtime_load_failed", if exit code != 2, if Config.json_path were not nil, or if the details.message lacked the `mix archive.uninstall mutagen_ex` troubleshooting copy. For r16's no-op-on-dep-adoption discipline, the success test at test/mix/tasks/mutagen_test.exs:79 seeds a single {:ok, _} and refute_received's on IO; entering the repair branch would either skip the short-circuit or call CapturingIoStub.emit which would `send(nil, ...)` and crash the test (no :capture_target set), so the test does catch a wrong-direction regression. Source-claim trace verified: spec r16 names Mix.Local.append_archives/0 (lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:269), Mix.path_for(:archives) (:314), Mix.Local.archive_ebin/1 (:318), :code.add_pathz/1 (:322) — all four live on the maybe_repair_archive_context → add_first_mutagen_archive_ebin call chain, not dead code. Out-of-Scope (intent) re-checked: mix.exs untouched, no env/config gate (gate is solely failure-tuple shape), test/integration/downstream_adoption_test.exs untouched, public surface of run/1 and run/2 unchanged (only @doc false __ensure_runtime__/1 added per ticket-authorized seam), only one new abort_reason atom (:runtime_load_failed). Production exit reaches non-zero via DefaultIo.emit/3 halting; the {:aborted, ...} return is the test-observable analogue, consistent with the rest of the module. Verifier's advisory Note about JsonReporter's @type abort_reason enum missing :runtime_load_failed is correctly downgraded — runtime contract holds (emit_error only guards is_atom/1), and lib/mutagen_ex/json_reporter.ex is explicitly Out-of-Scope (files). The :non_existing predicate clauses (lines 308–309) are not directly exercised by unit tests (only the {_, ~c"mutagen_ex.app"} clause is), but the ticket Verification enumerates exactly the two test cases that were written, so the deliverable matches scope; the full predicate is exercised end-to-end by S2's archive_install_test.exs which owns v11.execute=true.
APPROVED. Merged into think-pvr/archive-install-fix. Closing per orchestrator sign-off.

title: S1: Runtime preamble repair + mutagen.cli.r16 (archive-context self-heal)
